description_long: This is a vintage-style portable radio receiver with a rectangular body, predominantly green on the front and sides, and a black back panel. The front features a large circular speaker grille on the right, two small dials or knobs on the top, and a rectangular area that may serve as a display or tuning window. The radio has a prominent red handle arching over the top, which appears to be made of plastic or painted metal. The surface shows signs of wear and weathering, giving it a rugged, utilitarian look suitable for outdoor or urban use. The overall design is compact, with visible seams and edges, and the controls are easily accessible from the front and top.
description: A weathered, green and black portable radio receiver with a red handle, large speaker grille, and two top-mounted knobs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the green face, large round speaker grille, rectangular display area, and red handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile, red handle, and one of the control knobs protruding from the side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid black back panel and the tops of the two control knobs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the green side panel and the handle attachment point.
